title: useThrottle: rate-limit value updates
scenario_context: Scroll position updates 60 times/sec. Network requests off scroll need throttling to 4Hz.
verification_chain: Layer 7: throttled value updates at most every N ms
Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No trailing edge — last value lost
Throttling the callback not the value
what_breaks_naive_approach: Without throttle: 60 setStates/sec, parent re-renders cascade. With throttle: smooth + responsive.
reasoning: Track lastUpdate timestamp. If now-last >= delay, update immediately. Else schedule trailing update.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for generic_react_hooks; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: generic_react_hooks_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-181, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Throttle != debounce. 2) Trailing edge for final value. 3) Cleanup.

title: useFocusTrap for modals and dialogs
scenario_context: Modal opens; Tab should cycle within modal, not into background.
verification_chain: Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
what_breaks_naive_approach: Screen reader users navigate into background page.
reasoning: On mount, query focusables. Tab/Shift+Tab cycles. Restore previous active on unmount.

title: Health check endpoints: liveness vs readiness
scenario_context: Kubernetes probes need /live (process up) and /ready (can serve traffic). Different semantics.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Probes that wake on every request
what_breaks_naive_approach: DB down → readiness fails → K8s restarts pod (doesn't help, DB still down).
reasoning: Live = is JS event loop alive. Ready = can serve (DB connected, migrations applied). Restart-policy differs.
frontend_solution:
// Frontend contract for e2e-184: render the user-visible state, drive it through real interactions, and assert accessible output.
// Prefer role/name selectors, wait for network-visible state changes, and cover loading/error/empty/success paths without relying on implementation details.

what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Live vs ready distinction. 2) K8s lifecycle alignment. 3) No auth on probes.

title: Error boundary that reports + offers recovery via app router
scenario_context: Error in route component; show fallback with 'Go to dashboard' that uses router not full reload.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Full reload to recover — loses state
what_breaks_naive_approach: User stuck on error page; reload loses unrelated state.
reasoning: resetKey={pathname}; navigation re-renders with new key, boundary clears. Recovery actions navigate within router.

title: Discriminated union for paginated list responses
scenario_context: List API may return empty/has-next/last-page distinctly. Discriminated union forces handling.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User loads page; empty array — was it 'no data' or 'last page'?
reasoning: Variants: {kind: 'empty'}, {kind: 'page', items, nextCursor}, {kind: 'last', items}. Exhaustive switch.

title: What if migration fails partway — atomic + rollback
scenario_context: DB migration adds column + backfill. Halfway through, container OOMs. Need: idempotent migration that resumes.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Single transaction for huge backfill — locks
reasoning: Migration in chunks. State table records progress. Re-run continues from last checkpoint.
